commit.message:
refactor: unified-web-skill v3.0 — 6-engine architecture

Complete rewrite integrating 6 web engines under unified MCP interface:

Architecture:
- Engine Protocol with Capability enum (FETCH/SEARCH/INTERACT/STRUCTURED)
- EngineManager with SmartRouter + 5-layer fallback chain
- SiteRegistry: 67+ built-in sites with auto engine matching
- HealthMonitor: per-engine circuit breaker (closed→open→half-open)
- IntentClassifier: 9 query intent types, 130+ keyword patterns
- ResearchPipeline v3: intent→expand→discover→fetch→extract→quality→dedup→save

6 Engines:
- OpenCLI (100+ site adapters, structured data)
- Scrapling (3-tier HTTP: Fetcher→AsyncFetcher→StealthyFetcher)
- Lightpanda (Zig CDP browser, 9x speed, 16x less memory)
- PinchTab (remote browser MCP, session reuse)
- bb-browser (126+ adapters, most capable: FETCH+SEARCH+INTERACT+STRUCTURED)
- CLIBrowser (Rust stealth binary, ultimate fallback)

7 MCP Tools:
- research_and_collect (full pipeline, backward compatible)
- web_fetch (single URL, auto routing)
- web_cli (direct site commands)
- web_interact (browser automation)
- web_search (NEW: multi-engine search)
- web_crawl (NEW: BFS link-following crawl)
- engine_status (NEW: health check)

Tests: 327 passed, 19 skipped, 0 failures
Docs: README.md + docs/architecture.md + docs/api.md + docs/engines.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: set distDir='../.next' on Vercel to match post-build validator path

Vercel's post-build validator (introduced in recent @vercel/next update)
looks for routes-manifest-deterministic.json at /vercel/path0/.next/.
With rootDirectory:frontend, the build runs from /vercel/path0/frontend/
and outputs to frontend/.next — one directory below where the validator
looks. Setting distDir='../.next' when VERCEL=1 redirects the build output
to /vercel/path0/.next/ so the validator finds the file.

Locally VERCEL env var is not set so distDir defaults to .next (normal).

Also squashes the empty trigger commit into this real fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: word-wrap LLM prose at 80 chars, preserve tool output newlines

Two streaming modes:
- WordWrapStreamer: buffers LLM tokens, strips newlines, flushes at
  last word boundary before 80 chars. Each line gets prefix once.
- LineStreamer: flushes on \n for deterministic tool output (unused
  in current event handlers but available for future use).

Tool results still use truncate() for compact single-line display.

commit.message:
fix(docker): use **/dist/ glob to exclude all nested dist dirs from build context

dist/ only excluded the top-level folder, not apps/*/dist or packages/*/dist.
This caused stale compiled JS to be copied into every Docker build, and
tsc --build (incremental) skipped recompilation since outputs already existed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: replace 1-hour JWT auth tokens with permanent HMAC autologin codes

Newsletter links now use HMAC-based autologin codes (?ac=<hex>) instead of
Firebase custom tokens (?at=<jwt>). Key improvements:

- Never expires: HMAC code is deterministic, client exchanges it for a fresh
  Firebase token on-demand via Cloud Function API call
- Much shorter URLs: 67 chars (HMAC hex) vs 793 chars (JWT) — well under
  Mailgun's 1000-char click tracking limit
- No Firebase Admin SDK needed at send time: pure HMAC, no async token
  generation per subscriber

Changes:
- scripts/send-newsletter.mjs: Replace getOrCreateCustomToken() with
  generateAutologinCode() using HMAC('autologin:'+email, secret)
- functions/src/newsletterSubscriptionManagement.js: Add exchange_auth_code
  action that validates HMAC and returns fresh createCustomToken()
- functions/index.js: Support JSON responses from exchange_auth_code
- functions/src/newsletterConfirmationEmail.js: Remove JWT from confirmation
  URLs (Cloud Function already returns token in API response)
- App.tsx: Auto-login useEffect exchanges ac code via API; action handler
  supports ac for unsubscribe/resubscribe; skip auto-login for confirm action
- services/newsletterSubscribers.ts: Add exchangeNewsletterAuthCode()
- services/authService.ts: Check ac param in shouldWaitForAuth()
- build-plugins/constants.ts: Add ac to SPA redirect script

Backwards compatible: legacy at/authToken params still work for old emails.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[test][eventgrid-namespaces] fix assertion on number of received events (#37962)

This test failed most of the time because only one event is received.

according to the doc it is expected:

> /** Max wait time value for receive operation in Seconds. It is the
time in seconds that the server approximately waits for the availability
of an event and responds to the request. If an event is available, the
broker responds immediately to the client. Minimum value is 10 seconds,
while maximum value is 120 seconds. If not specified, the default value
is 60 seconds. */
  maxWaitTime?: number;

This PR update the assertion to check that number of received messages
is between 1 and 2.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
watchdog: distinguish RPC busy from de-registration; add audit sections 14.11-14.12

- RPC timeout no longer immediately counts as de-registration.
  If timed has logged within DAEMON_ACTIVE_SECS (default 90s), the
  daemon is alive but busy (fork resolution, reconnection storm).
  Busy timeouts increment rpc_busy_streak; only after RPC_BUSY_MAX
  (default 10 consecutive polls, ~90s) without log activity does it
  escalate to a fail_streak increment and eventual restart.
- When busy: logs '⏳ RPC timeout — daemon is alive and logging
  (busy_streak: N/10); NOT counting as de-registration'
- When silent AND unresponsive: logs 'RPC call failed — daemon has
  NOT logged in 90s' and escalates normally.
- RPC_TIMEOUT default: 3s → 8s (more headroom during load spikes)
- FAIL_THRESHOLD default: 2 → 3 (confirmed not-active before restart)
- daemon_recently_active() function added (journalctl short-unix parse)
- rpc_busy_streak state variable + reset on RPC success
- Restart log message: 'detected' → 'confirmed' to reduce false alarm tone
- New --rpc-busy-max N and --daemon-active-secs N CLI flags
- docs: add sections 14.11 (tokio starvation via reconnection storm)
  and 14.12 (watchdog false-restart on RPC timeout) to security audit;
  add three new rows to summary attack surface table

Fixes the pattern seen Apr 8 2026: restarts #12-17 at 10-min intervals
triggered by RPC starvation from the fork-resolution busy-loop.

Fix CI: use raw deno commands instead of deno task

run from a sub-directory, causing the chained cd commands to hit

feat: UX improvements, savings rate variability, chart fixes

1. HICP → 'Inflacja CPI': removed jargon label in InputPanel and
   VerdictBanner; Eurostat source still shown in brackets

2. Base scenario = 0% (neutral, current price): HMM path was giving p50
   as base which was often negative; base is now always {deltaStock:0,
   deltaFx:0} — a neutral 'hold current position' reference point

3. Timeline chart full-width and color fix:
   - Base line changed from amber to blue (#2563eb) — distinct from the
     orange purchasing-power line which is now slate (#94a3b8, dashed)
   - Chart height increased 300 → 420px
   - Bear/Bull labels in legend now read 'Bear (pesymistyczny)' etc.
   - Timeline moved below ComparisonChart as full-width section

4. Historical analysis card rewritten for intermediate investors:
   - Removed Greek symbol badges (σ, ρ); replaced with plain Polish labels
   - Expanded section now shows metric cards with values + interpretations
   - HMM regime block uses plain language (Faza wzrostów/spadków)
   - Methodology explanation in plain Polish at bottom

5. Savings rate mean-reversion:
   - Added blendedSavingsRate() to inflationProjection.ts
     (equilibrium 3.0%, tau=24 months — savings track NBP reference rate)
   - App.tsx computes effectiveSavingsRate and passes to CalcInputs
   - InputPanel shows a note when effective rate differs from nominal
     by more than 0.05pp (e.g. '3.85% → effective avg 3.42%')

commit.message:
Feat/phase 3 ecommerce (#7)

* feat: implement Phase 3 e-commerce heuristics and related product pruning

* docs: modernize OUTPUT_CONTRACT and EXTRACTION wiki references

* feat(core): modernize extraction engine, eradicate panics, and add cloud jina opt-in

This commit resolves major architectural goals by hardening execution stability,
improving text-extraction heuristics, and enforcing privacy-by-default routing:

1. Panic Eradication & Error Handling
   - System-wide removal of `unwrap()` and `expect()`, replacing them with safe
     `Result` propagation.
   - Refactored `RipwebError` and domain errors (`DdgError`, `GithubError`, etc.)
     to leverage the `thiserror` crate.
   - Updated `main()` to return `anyhow::Result<()>`, migrating `std::process::exit()`
     calls to graceful `anyhow::bail!` error bubbling.

2. AST-Based Semantic Pruning
   - Replaced fragile character-counting link saturation heuristics with mathematical
     AST calculations via the `tl` crate (`evaluate_block_saturation`).
   - Accurately computes link density by evaluating structural DOM nodes,
     significantly improving the accuracy of sidebar and navigation pruning.

3. Privacy by Default
   - Added an explicit `--allow-cloud` CLI flag to gate third-party
     extraction proxies (Jina.ai).
   - Forces `ripweb` to strictly adhere to localized parsing and heuristic fallbacks
     unless explicitly given permission to use offsite JS rendering engines.

Also updates `CURRENT_PRIORITIES.md` to mark these milestones as completed.

* fix(ci): repair PR #7 branch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Switch scheduled Pages deployment from concurrent git pushes to single artifact-based deploy (#16)

* Initial plan

* Switch scheduled workflow to artifact-based GitHub Pages deployment

Replace concurrent gh-pages git pushes with the recommended Actions
Pages pattern: build jobs run in parallel and upload artifacts, a
single deploy job downloads all artifacts and publishes once via
actions/configure-pages + actions/upload-pages-artifact +
actions/deploy-pages.

- schedule.yaml: set permissions (pages:write, id-token:write,
  contents:read), add concurrency group, pass publish:false to build
  jobs, add single deploy job
- build.yaml: update permissions, replace git-push publish job with
  deploy-pages-based publish (for standalone workflow_dispatch use)

commit.message:
Enforce exclusive score bounds in grader and inference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

EXP-1291-1300: AID-deconfounded therapy assessment with precondition framework

10 experiments assessing therapy settings with physics-based precondition
gating and AID deconfounding:

- EXP-1291: Deconfounded ISF (loop dampens corrections, ratio 2.72→3.62×)
- EXP-1292: Quantified basal recs (7/11 decrease, mean -16.2%)
- EXP-1293-1294: Balance simulation and time-block ISF
- EXP-1295: Bolus timing (mean -7.7min, 35.5% pre-bolus)
- EXP-1296: Fasting trends (3 too low, 2 too high, 3 appropriate)
- EXP-1297: Weekly report cards (mean composite 60.4/100)
- EXP-1298: Correction validation (6/11 ISF too aggressive)
- EXP-1299-1300: ICR analysis and integrated assessment (51.4/100)

Precondition framework incorporates prior thresholds:
- EXP-492: RMSE quality gates (<3 good, >10 poor)
- EXP-454: Conservation integral (<15 mg·h adequate)
- EXP-490: ISF consistency (IQR/median < 0.5)
- 5/11 patients fail ≥1 precondition (h: 35.8% CGM, i: R²=-2.77)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Document dependency CVE policy in SECURITY.md

Clarify that a dependency having a CVE does not mean gh has a
vulnerability. We use govulncheck for reachability analysis and
ask reporters to demonstrate impact before we act on dependency CVE
reports.

commit.message:
fix(api): force clean tsc build to prevent stale incremental cache in Docker

tsc --build with composite+incremental reuses .tsbuildinfo from prior
builds. In Docker, this caused ai.js to keep the old router.all('/*')
even after the source was fixed. Force clean dist before build and add
--force flag so tsc always recompiles from scratch.

commit.message:
fix(smoketest): prevent SIGPIPE from trivy killing script via EXIT trap

trivy --version outputs many lines; head -1 closes the pipe early causing
trivy to get SIGPIPE (exit 141). With set -eo pipefail this non-zero exit
fires the EXIT trap inside the $() subshell which calls cleanup/exit 0,
terminating the whole script. Add '|| true' to suppress the SIGPIPE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

so the overlay-scrollbar track is reachable and clickable on macOS.

#267 – UserBadge now eagerly applies server-loaded theme and dark-mode
preferences via useEffect hooks on mount, so the colour palette and
light/dark state are correct immediately instead of only after the
user opens the account menu.

#268 – Expand leiValidSortFields allowlist from 8 to all 36 actual DB
columns exposed by the LEI records UI; extract to package-level var for
testability; add two unit tests: TestLeiValidSortFields_AllowsExpectedColumns
and TestLeiValidSortFields_RejectsVirtualAndInjectionAttempts.

---------

commit.message:
Fix CI: use working-directory instead of chained cd commands

The chained 'cd && deno task' shell strings exceeded Linux's argument
list limit (E2BIG). Use separate steps with working-directory instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add autotune & UAM characterization: oref0 vs physics ML

Evaluate and compare UAM detection and autotune parameter optimization
across oref0/AAPS/Trio and physics-based ML (cgmencode) on 10 patients
with ~180 days of continuous Nightscout data each.

Key findings:
- UAM: Physics ML F1=0.513 vs oref0 F1=0.344 (9/10 patient wins)
- Physics ML: higher precision (0.567 vs 0.338), similar recall
- oref0: longer lead time (38 min vs 29 min) but more false positives
- 68% of glucose rise events have no carb entry (fundamental challenge)
- Effective ISF = 1.36x profile ISF (AID compensation masks settings)
- Patients d,e,i show >2x ISF mismatch (profile too aggressive)
- Loop runs at scheduled basal only 3-77% of the time

Deliverables:
- tools/aid-autoresearch/eval_autotune_uam.py (evaluation pipeline)
- docs/60-research/autotune-uam-characterization-report.md (full report)
- visualizations/autotune-uam-eval/ (6 figures + raw JSON results)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove cloud-init, make deploy script fully idempotent

- Delete cloud-init.yaml and remove custom_data from vm.tf
- Remove 10 Terraform variables only used for cloud-init templating
- Simplify deploy-infrastructure KV fetch and terraform plan
- Rewrite deploy-application SSH script with idempotent checks:
  Docker installed? Git installed? Repo cloned? Each step checks
  its own precondition and acts accordingly.

commit.message:
fix: add OpenEnv root reset and step aliases

Expose OpenEnv-compatible root endpoints (/reset, /step, /state/{episode_id}) and API aliases (/api/reset, /api/step, /api/state/{episode_id}) to prevent 405 on evaluator probes.

Add endpoint coercion for relaxed action payloads and tests for alias behavior.
